# Sub 2 Phase 2 — REM run/stage state model JSONL writer + per-phase wall-clock + cadence-overflow detection

## Parent context

Epic #12065 Sub 2 #12068 — Phase 2 follow-up to PR #12081 (Phase 1a) + Phase 1b ticket. Phase 1a shipped the 5 axis-count helpers; Phase 1b exposes them via MCP. Phase 2 ships the per-cycle JSONL state model that captures run/stage outcome + per-phase wall-clock + cadence-overflow detection (operator-framed regression-prevention substrate).

## Dependency

**BLOCKED-ON:**
1. PR #12081 merge (Phase 1a helpers must exist for state model to invoke them)
2. Phase 1b PR merge (MCP exposure scaffold must exist for AC-WallClock-4 trending surface)

## Operator framing (anchor)

@tobiu 2026-05-27 ~08:00Z:
> *"if we trigger the process / task inside the orchestrator every hour => if it e.g. was 10m before and now magically grew to 90m (>the 60m repeat window) => trouble."*

Per-phase wall-clock + cadence-overflow detection is the regression-prevention substrate for this exact failure mode.

## Contract Ledger

Per `ticket-intake-workflow.md §7` — documented upfront per ticket-author discipline.

### JSONL state model schema

Storage: append-only JSONL at `.neo-ai-data/rem-runs/<runId>.jsonl` (durable across restart; queryable post-incident). Per Discussion #12062 §2.11 + scope-extended per #12068 comment 4552584285.

```js
// Cycle-level entry (one per cycle invocation)
{
  runId                 : String,   // crypto.randomUUID()
  reason                : String,   // 'manual' | 'orchestrator-periodic' | 'startup-auto'
  startedAt             : Number,   // epoch ms
  completedAt           : Number,   // epoch ms
  wallClockMs           : Number,   // completedAt - startedAt (derived; convenience field)
  configuredCadenceMs   : Number,   // aiConfig.orchestrator.intervals.dreamMs (resolved at cycle start)
  cycleOverflowSignal   : Boolean,  // wallClockMs > configuredCadenceMs * overflowThreshold (default 0.8)
  cycleOverflowRatio    : Number,   // wallClockMs / configuredCadenceMs — sortable trending signal
  outcome               : String,   // 'completed' | 'skipped' | 'failed'
  reasonCode            : String,   // 'ok' | 'no-undigested-sessions' | 'already-processing' | 'provider-unreachable' | 'backpressure' | 'lease-held' | 'extraction-failed' | 'topology-failed' | 'lazy-edge-drain-failed'
  failurePhase          : String,   // populated when outcome === 'failed'
  failureReason         : String,
  lastSuccessfulPhase   : String,
  cycleScopePhases      : [String], // ordered list of phases attempted
  perPhaseStates        : [
    {
      phase            : String,    // e.g. 'providerReady', 'sessionQuery', 'memorySessionIngest', 'triVector', 'topology', 'gapSession', 'lazyEdges', 'garbageCollection', 'goldenPath'
      startedAt        : Number,
      completedAt      : Number,
      wallClockMs      : Number,
      status           : String,    // 'completed' | 'skipped' | 'failed'
      details          : Object     // phase-specific telemetry
    }
  ],
  perSessionStates      : [
    {
      sessionId        : String,
      payloadSizeTokens: Number,
      memorySessionIngest: {status, errorReasons},
      triVector        : {status, attempts, errorKind?},
      topology         : {status, conflictCount},
      gapSession       : {status},
      graphDigestedFlag: Boolean,
      failureReasons   : [String]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Wall-clock + cadence-overflow contract

| Surface | Behavior | Source of Authority |
|---------|----------|---------------------|
| `wallClockMs` per phase | Derived from `completedAt - startedAt`; reported even when phase status is `skipped` or `failed` (zero for instant-skip) | This ticket |
| `wallClockMs` cycle-level | Derived cumulative; reported on cycle completion regardless of outcome | This ticket |
| `cycleOverflowSignal` | `true` when `wallClockMs > configuredCadenceMs * overflowThreshold` (default `0.8`); `overflowThreshold` is configurable per `aiConfig.orchestrator.intervals.dreamOverflowThreshold` (default `0.8`) | Operator framing; conservative 80% threshold gives early-warning before actual overlap |
| `cycleOverflowRatio` | `wallClockMs / configuredCadenceMs`; sortable trending signal for MCP query | This ticket |
| WARN log on overflow | `[Orchestrator] REM cycle wall-clock <wallClockMs>ms exceeded <threshold>% of configured cadence <configuredCadenceMs>ms — back-to-back overlap risk` | Operator framing |

### MCP exposure delta (extends Phase 1b)

| Surface | Change | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `get_rem_pipeline_state` MCP tool from Phase 1b | EXTENDED — adds `recentCycles` field with array of last N cycles' `{runId, wallClockMs, cycleOverflowSignal, cycleOverflowRatio, outcome}` for trending visibility | Trending degradation visible before crossing cadence boundary |

### Retention

| Surface | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| JSONL files | Append-only per-cycle; one file per `runId`; no rotation in Phase 2 (Phase 3 if needed) |
| Disk cap | Documented but not enforced in Phase 2; if file count grows large, operator runs the standard backup/defrag tooling |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** JSONL state model writer ships — invoked from either `processUndigestedSessions` (current production path) OR Sub 3 `executeRemCycle` if landed first
- [ ] **AC2:** Per-phase + cycle-level `wallClockMs` derived from epoch-ms timestamps; reported regardless of outcome
- [ ] **AC3:** Per-session state model captures payload size + per-stage outcome (memorySessionIngest, triVector, topology, gapSession, graphDigestedFlag)
- [ ] **AC4:** Topology outcome included in state model (closes silent-failure surface of PR #12077 runbook hypothesis #10 — TopologyInferenceEngine void-return ambiguity)
- [ ] **AC-WallClock-1:** Per-phase entries include `startedAt` + `completedAt` + derived `wallClockMs`
- [ ] **AC-WallClock-2:** Cycle entry includes cumulative `wallClockMs` + `configuredCadenceMs` + derived `cycleOverflowSignal` + `cycleOverflowRatio`
- [ ] **AC-WallClock-3:** WARN log when `cycleOverflowSignal: true` fires — operator-visible signal before back-to-back overlap actually happens
- [ ] **AC-WallClock-4:** Phase 1b MCP `get_rem_pipeline_state` tool extended with `recentCycles` field surfacing wall-clock + overflow trending
- [ ] **AC-WallClock-5:** Unit test seeds fake state model with `wallClockMs > configuredCadenceMs * 0.8` + asserts `cycleOverflowSignal: true` + WARN log emitted (mocked logger)
- [ ] **AC6:** Documentation update at `learn/agentos/rem-state-model.md` (created in Phase 1b) appended with state-model schema + cadence-overflow semantics + operator-cookbook examples
- [ ] **AC7:** Live re-measurement of 5 axes + wall-clock trending against operator's production substrate post-merge (operator-side empirical validation)

## Avoided Traps

- ❌ Don't ship Phase 2 BEFORE Phase 1a (#12081) + Phase 1b merge — schema readers depend on the helpers + the MCP tool
- ❌ Don't enforce JSONL disk cap in Phase 2 — adds operator-policy concerns out of scope; defer to Phase 3 if file count grows
- ❌ Don't bundle Sub 3 `executeRemCycle` integration into this ticket — Sub 3 #12069 owns that integration; this Phase 2 ships the state-model writer + invocation from current `processUndigestedSessions`; Sub 3 absorbs/extends later
- ❌ Don't use graph nodes for state model — graph mutations during REM run risk active-control-plane collateral damage per Discussion #12062 §2.10. JSONL files are off-graph + durable.
- ❌ Don't auto-rotate / auto-purge JSONL — same operator-policy boundary as backups; manual tooling existing per `ai:backup` family

## Operator framing locked

> *"if we trigger the process / task inside the orchestrator every hour => if it e.g. was 10m before and now magically grew to 90m (>the 60m repeat window) => trouble."* — @tobiu 2026-05-27 ~08:00Z

This ticket is the regression-prevention substrate.

- 2026-05-27T14:24:07Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `8261800` - "feat(orchestrator): unified executeRemCycle() with typed cycle outcome (#12069)

Adds the keystone Sub 3 method consolidating the Sandman REM pipeline behind a
single canonical entrypoint that returns a typed outcome envelope. This is the
silent-failure fix at the orchestrator boundary: the periodic dream path used
to map every non-throwing return from DreamService.processUndigestedSessions()
to `completed`, hiding zero-session no-ops, concurrent-invocation guards, and
provider-unreachable early returns. The new method discriminates `completed`
from `skipped` from `failed` from the start.

Outcome envelope:
- `completed` -- provider ready, undigested sessions existed, processing
   finished without throw. `sessionsProcessed` carries the pre-call count.
- `skipped`   -- ran successfully but did no work (concurrent-invocation
   guard, zero undigested sessions, dry-run, etc.). `skipReason` populated.
- `failed`    -- provider readiness gate rejected OR an in-pipeline throw was
   caught. Either `diagnostic` (provider failure) or `error` (throws) populated.

Mitigations from the Sub 1 forensics runbook applied inline:
- H1: dreamService.isProcessing now surfaces as `skipped/concurrent-invocation`
  rather than DreamService's previous debug-only log line.
- H2: provider readiness check resolves the graph provider explicitly via the
  Sub 6 substrate (runProviderReadinessGate from the same orchestrator class),
  not the generic modelProvider check that DreamService still performs internally.
- H3: typed outcome eliminates the unconditional `completed` mapping; the
  orchestrator dream callback now switches on outcome.status and routes
  recordTaskOutcome through the matching state.

Dream task refactor: the periodic dream callback at the cadence-engine
boundary now delegates to executeRemCycle() and maps the typed outcome to
healthService.recordTaskOutcome. The MaintenanceBackpressureService wrapper
continues to own the heavy-maintenance lease; executeRemCycle is intentionally
lease-agnostic so the standalone Sandman CLI runner (Sub 4 follow-up) can
delegate without double-leasing.

Deferred per scope-restraint:
- AC3 (Sub 2 Part B JSONL state model wiring) waits for the #12088 writer
- AC5 (Sub 7 chunking-aware activation) waits for the #12073 strategy
- AC6 (runSandman CLI delegation refactor) is Sub 4 (#12070) territory
- AC7 (full silent-failure regression suite) is Sub 9 (#12075); this PR
  ships the typed-outcome contract coverage only

Tests: 9 new unit cases lock the outcome contract -- provider-gate failure
populates diagnostic, processUndigestedSessions throws populate error, the
findUndigestedSessions throw path is covered separately, sessionsProcessed
carries the pre-call count, runId is unique per call, reason + mode survive
the envelope."

* feat(orchestrator): unified executeRemCycle() with typed cycle outcome (#12069)

Adds the keystone Sub 3 method consolidating the Sandman REM pipeline behind a
single canonical entrypoint that returns a typed outcome envelope. This is the
silent-failure fix at the orchestrator boundary: the periodic dream path used
to map every non-throwing return from DreamService.processUndigestedSessions()

provider-unreachable early returns. The new method discriminates `completed`
from `skipped` from `failed` from the start.

Outcome envelope:
- `completed` -- provider ready, undigested sessions existed, processing
   finished without throw. `sessionsProcessed` carries the pre-call count.
- `skipped`   -- ran successfully but did no work (concurrent-invocation
   guard, zero undigested sessions, dry-run, etc.). `skipReason` populated.
- `failed`    -- provider readiness gate rejected OR an in-pipeline throw was
   caught. Either `diagnostic` (provider failure) or `error` (throws) populated.

* refactor(orchestrator): move executeRemCycle + readiness gate to DreamService (#12069)

Operator-direct architectural correction. PR #12092 (already merged) and the
prior PR #12096 commit both added large methods directly to Orchestrator.mjs,
contradicting the recent service-decomposition refactor that thinned the
orchestrator into a coordinator + delegated logic to dedicated service files
(MaintenanceBackpressureService, CadenceEngine, DreamService, etc.).

DreamService is the natural home for executeRemCycle: it already owns the REM
pipeline (processUndigestedSessions, findUndigestedSessions, etc.), so the
typed-outcome unified entrypoint composes existing methods on `this`. The
checkProviderReadiness helper moves with it -- both depend on the runSandman
helpers (waitForProvider, createProviderFailureDiagnostic,
getGraphProviderReadinessTarget) which DreamService now imports directly.

Net effect on Orchestrator.mjs:
- DELETE async executeRemCycle({...}) and its JSDoc (~110 LOC body)
- DELETE async runProviderReadinessGate() and its JSDoc (~50 LOC body)
- DELETE imports: GraphService, createProviderFailureDiagnostic,
  getGraphProviderReadinessTarget, waitForProvider
- Refactor the periodic dream callback: stop calling local methods, delegate
  to this.dreamService.executeRemCycle({...}) and map the typed outcome to
  recordTaskOutcome
- Drive-by: fix the pre-existing `reason.reason` bug in the dream callback
  (CadenceEngine already unwraps the `{reason: '...'}` object via line 46 of
  CadenceEngine.mjs, so the callback receives the string directly -- matches
  the summary/kbSync pattern)

Net effect on DreamService.mjs:
- ADD async executeRemCycle({reason, mode, includeGoldenPath, includeDecay,
  dryRun}) returning the typed outcome envelope (completed | skipped | failed)
- ADD async checkProviderReadiness() returning {ready, diagnostic?}
- ADD imports: AiConfig + the three runSandman readiness helpers

Tests:
- DELETE Orchestrator.executeRemCycle.spec.mjs (orchestrator scope)
- ADD services/DreamService.executeRemCycle.spec.mjs with the same 9 typed-
  outcome cases, now stubbing the singleton via the beforeEach/afterEach
  swap pattern (DreamService is a Neo.setupClass singleton instance)
- Update Orchestrator.spec.mjs consumer-path case to stub
  dreamService.executeRemCycle directly + assert the orchestrator passes
  the right options envelope + maps the typed `failed` outcome to
  recordTaskOutcome with failurePhase: 'provider-readiness' + diagnostic

* refactor(orchestrator): drop includeGoldenPath no-op + add intent JSDoc (#12069 cycle-3)

Addresses Codex review on PR #12096 cycle-2. Three required actions:

1. Remove the no-op `includeGoldenPath` knob. The flag accepted but never did
   anything except log "deferred when set"; that exposed an API option that
   does not match its name and encoded board sequencing into runtime API.
   Removed from `DreamService.executeRemCycle()` signature, JSDoc, orchestrator
   caller, and the consumer-path test assertion. The dedicated golden-path
   refresh helper lands in its own future change when the behavior exists.

2. Replace source archaeology with durable intent documentation.
   - `@summary` added to both new public methods on `DreamService`
     (`executeRemCycle`, `checkProviderReadiness`).
   - Intent comments added inline for the four important branch groups
     in `executeRemCycle()`: provider gate, dry-run short-circuit,
     concurrent-invocation guard, pre-count query, no-work path, work path.
     Comments describe what the branch is for and why it lives there, not
     project-board sequencing.
   - Removed the "Sub 5 refresh helper" and "deferred to its own ticket"
     wording from method docs and the runtime log line.

3. PR body update (separate `gh pr edit` follow-up) reflects the cycle-2 + 3
   reality: DreamService owns `executeRemCycle`; Orchestrator delegates;
   evidence is 10/10 focused tests; pre-rewrite stack reference removed.
